steps.action: Confirm Python 3.8 through 3.13, install ngrok, then install Flask and the Twilio SDK with pip install flask twilio.
steps.success_signal: ngrok is available and pip installs both Flask and Twilio without error.

evidence.excerpt: Install Python (link takes you to an external page) (3.8–3.13). See supported versions (link takes you to an external page) . steps.action: Obtain an SMS-capable Twilio phone number from the Console.
steps.success_signal: The number appears in the account and can be selected by the Messaging quickstart.

evidence.excerpt: Twilio Virtual Phone lets you try Twilio quickly regardless of your country's regulations for messaging mobile handsets. To message a mobile handset, see Send SMS and MMS messages . Open the Send an SMS page in the Twilio Console (link takes you to an external page) . On the Send to Virtual Phone tab, select the number

number . In the Account Dashboard , copy your Account SID and Auth Token and paste them in a temporary local file for use later in this quickstart. steps.action: Copy the Account SID and Auth Token from the Twilio Console.
steps.success_signal: Both credential values are available for local configuration.
notes: No literal official-doc excerpt supports the step's success_signal.

steps.action: Store the SID and token as TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID and TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN environment variables.
steps.success_signal: The Python process can read both variables.

evidence.excerpt: environment variables. Replace <your-account-sid> and <your-auth-token> with your Account SID and Auth Token: Copy code block 1 export TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID =< your-account-sid > 2 export TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN =< your-auth-token > To learn more, see Store
